title: Add "use secondary for accent" toggle to School metaobject
description:
Some schools have a primary color that's too dark for buttons/accents on PDP and other pages. Add a boolean field  to the School metaobject so we can control per-school whether  pulls from  or .

**Affected sections (all places that read  for accent/theming):**
-  —  on the product page
-  —  on the features banner
-  —  on collection pages (coming-soon + school-header + product grid)
-  —  on cart line items
-  — JS-based  for coming-soon fallback
-  — JSON school map color for badge text
-  — dot fallback color when no logo

**Approach:**
1. Add  boolean field to the School metaobject definition in Shopify Admin
2. Create a reusable snippet that resolves the accent color based on the toggle
3. Update all 7 files to use the resolved accent color instead of hardcoding 
4. Set the toggle for schools identified in the color reference table (Alabama, Auburn, Utah State, Maine, Charlotte, Appalachian State, Southern Miss)

title: Add  boolean field to School metaobject in Shopify Admin
description:
Manual step in Shopify Admin:

1. Go to Settings → Custom data → Metaobjects → School
2. Add a new field:
   - Name: "Use secondary for accent"
   - Handle: 
   - Type: True/False (boolean)
   - Description: "When enabled, PDP buttons and accent colors use the secondary color instead of primary"
3. Save the metaobject definition
4. Set to  for these schools (per the color reference table): Alabama, Auburn, Utah State, Maine, Charlotte, Appalachian State, Southern Miss

This must be done before the theme code changes can reference .
